title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) at home! This authentic recipe features fresh seafood, crisp scallions, and a savory dipping sauce. Perfect for a delicious meal.
lowCompetitionKeywords: Haemul Pajeon with shrimp and squid
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Perfect Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.imageAlt: A freshly cooked Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) on a plate with dipping sauce and chopsticks.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a crispy, savory, and authentic Haemul Pajeon, packed with fresh seafood and green onions.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved crispy Korean seafood and green onion pancake, a perfect blend of savory flavors and delightful textures. This traditional dish is packed with an assortment of fresh seafood, nestled within a light, savory batter and pan-fried to golden perfection. It makes an excellent appetizer or a satisfying main course, especially when paired with a tangy dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, clams, chopped into bite-sized pieces
about 8-10 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, minced garlic, and red pepper flakes if using. Set aside.

instructions.title: Mix the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, egg, and cold water (or anchovy broth). Whisk until just combined, ensuring there are no large lumps. Do not overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Ensure your mixed seafood is thawed, cleaned, and patted dry. Cut any larger pieces into bite-sized portions. Cut green onions into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili if using.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and add about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil. Pour a ladleful of batter into the hot pan to form a thin, round layer. Immediately arrange a portion of the green onions, sliced onions, and seafood evenly over the batter.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Pour a little more batter over the seafood and vegetables to bind them. Cook for 3-4 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges, then carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 3-4 minutes until the other side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. If using red chili, place slices on top after flipping.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Slide the cooked Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board and cut into wedges or squares. Repeat with the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, ensure your water is very cold and consider adding a small amount of sparkling water to the batter.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook one pancake at a time to ensure even browning and crispiness. Use a good non-stick pan.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispy texture. It pairs wonderfully with Korean makgeolli (rice wine).
article:
Haemul Pajeon, the iconic Korean seafood pancake, is a culinary delight that has captured the hearts and palates of food lovers worldwide. More than just a simple pancake, it’s a symphony of textures and flavors, combining the crispiness of a perfectly fried exterior with a tender, savory interior packed with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Whether you’ve encountered it sizzling hot at a bustling Korean street food stall, as a comforting appetizer in a restaurant, or heard whispers of its deliciousness, Haemul Pajeon offers an experience that is both authentic and incredibly satisfying. If you're searching for a reliable Haemul Pajeon recipe to recreate this magic at home, you've come to the right place. Get ready to dive into the world of this beloved Korean staple, learning its origins, key ingredients, and the secrets to making it perfectly crispy every time.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

At its core, Haemul Pajeon translates to "seafood scallion pancake." "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pajeon" refers to a pancake made primarily with "pa" (green onions or scallions). Unlike some other Korean pancakes which might feature kimchi (Kimchi-jeon) or mung beans (Bindaetteok), Haemul Pajeon distinguishes itself with its generous inclusion of various seafood elements.

The magic of Haemul Pajeon lies in its remarkable balance. The batter, typically made from flour, water (sometimes broth for added depth), and egg, creates a sturdy yet tender base. This base is then generously studded with long strands of green onions, providing a fresh, slightly pungent crunch, and an assortment of seafood like shrimp, squid, mussels, and oysters. When cooked, the edges become wonderfully crispy and golden brown, while the inside remains soft and moist, allowing the natural sweetness of the seafood and the freshness of the scallions to shine through. It’s a dish often enjoyed on a rainy day in Korea, paired with a bottle of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju, serving as both a hearty snack and a satisfying side dish.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon

Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon recipe starts with gathering the right ingredients. Freshness is key, especially when it comes to the seafood and green onions, which are the stars of this dish.

For the Pancake Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (for extra crispiness, you can use a mix of 3/4 cup all-purpose flour and 1/4 cup rice flour or Korean pancake mix)
*   1 cup cold water (or cold anchovy broth for enhanced umami flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Optional: 1/4 teaspoon baking powder (for a slightly lighter texture)

For the Seafood (Haemul):
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood, cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces. Popular choices include:
    *   Shrimp (peeled, deveined, roughly chopped)
    *   Squid (cleaned, body sliced into rings or small pieces)
    *   Mussels or clams (shelled, cleaned)
    *   Oysters (fresh or frozen, thawed)
*   You can use fresh or frozen seafood; just ensure it's thoroughly thawed and patted dry.

For the Vegetables (Pajeon):
*   1 large bunch (about 10-12) green onions/scallions, trimmed and cut into 2-3 inch lengths (some for batter, some for topping)
*   1/4 medium onion, thinly sliced (optional, but adds sweetness)
*   1-2 fresh red or green chilies, thinly sliced (optional, for a touch of heat and color)
*   Optional: a handful of other julienned vegetables like carrots or bell peppers for color and crunch.

For Frying:
*   4-6 tablespoons vegetable oil, canola oil, or grapeseed oil (don't be shy with the oil!)

For the Dipping Sauce (See "Serving Suggestions" for full recipe):
*   Soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), minced garlic, sesame seeds.

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide

This step-by-step guide will walk you through the essential Haemul Pajeon recipe, ensuring you achieve that perfect balance of crispy and tender.

Step 1: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables
*   If using frozen seafood, thaw it completely and pat it very dry with paper towels to prevent a watery pancake. Chop larger pieces into bite-sized chunks.
*   Wash the green onions thoroughly. Cut about two-thirds of the green onions into 2-3 inch segments. Reserve the remaining one-third for arranging on top of the pancake during cooking.
*   Thinly slice the onion and chilies (if using).

Step 2: Make the Batter
*   In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and optional baking powder.
*   Gradually add the cold water (or broth) and egg, whisking until the batter is smooth and free of lumps. Be careful not to overmix, as this can develop the gluten too much and make the pancake tough. A few small lumps are fine. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to a crêpe batter, but a bit thicker.

Step 3: Combine Ingredients
*   Add the prepared seafood, the majority of the cut green onions, and the sliced onion and chilies (if using) to the batter. Gently fold everything together until just combined.

Step 4: Cook the Pancake
*   Heat a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. The oil should be shimmering hot, but not smoking.
*   Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Don't make it too thick; aim for about 1/2 to 3/4 inch thick.
*   Immediately arrange some of the reserved green onion segments and a few pieces of seafood on top of the pancake. This makes for an appealing presentation.
*   Cook for 4-6 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges start to look set. You might need to adjust the heat to prevent burning while ensuring it cooks through.
*   Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula (or two spatulas if it's very large). Add another tablespoon or two of oil around the edges of the pan to help crisp up the second side.
*   Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, or until golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked.
*   Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board.

Step 5: Slice and Serve
*   Slice the hot pancake into wedges or squares, similar to pizza.
*   Repeat the cooking process with the remaining batter.
*   Serve immediately with the dipping sauce.

## Tips for the Perfect Korean Seafood Pancake

Achieving that ideal crispy exterior and flavorful interior for your Haemul Pajeon recipe isn't difficult, but a few tips can elevate your results:

*   **Cold Liquids for Batter:** Using cold water or broth helps to inhibit gluten development, resulting in a more tender pancake. Some even use ice water.
*   **Don't Overmix:** Overmixing the batter can make your pancake chewy instead of tender. Mix just until combined.
*   **Plenty of Oil:** Don't be afraid of oil! Haemul Pajeon benefits from a generous amount of oil to get that signature crispy crust. Drizzle extra oil around the edges before flipping and during the second side cooking.
*   **High Heat (but watch it!):** Start with medium-high heat to get a good sizzle and crisp, then you might reduce it slightly to ensure the inside cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat thawed seafood very dry with paper towels before adding it to the batter.
*   **Anchovy Broth:** For an extra layer of authentic Korean flavor, substitute plain water with homemade anchovy broth (dashima-gukmul). It adds a subtle umami depth.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan when it’s at its crispiest.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

No Haemul Pajeon recipe is complete without a complementary dipping sauce. The sauce provides a tangy, savory, and sometimes spicy counterpoint to the rich pancake.

Simple Dipping Sauce Recipe:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional, for heat)
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   A pinch of chopped green onion (from the white part)

Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and mix well. Taste and adjust to your preference – more vinegar for tang, more soy sauce for saltiness, etc.

Haemul Pajeon is traditionally served as an appetizer or a snack. It’s a quintessential "rainy day food" in Korea, often enjoyed with makgeolli (a milky, lightly alcoholic rice wine) or soju. It also pairs wonderfully with Korean beer or simply as a light meal on its own. Cut into bite-sized wedges, it’s perfect for sharing.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations

While the classic Haemul Pajeon recipe is fantastic, its versatility allows for numerous delicious variations:

*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy and tangy kick, add about 1/2 cup of finely chopped, well-squeezed kimchi to your batter. Reduce the salt slightly as kimchi is already salty.
*   **Meat Pajeon (Gogi Pajeon):** Thinly sliced beef (bulgogi style) or pork can be added alongside or instead of seafood for a heartier pancake.
*   **Vegetarian Pajeon (Yachae Pajeon):** If seafood isn't your preference, you can make a delicious vegetable-only pancake. Load it up with sliced mushrooms, zucchini, carrots, bell peppers, and plenty of green onions.
*   **Cheese Pajeon:** A modern and popular twist involves sprinkling mozzarella cheese over the pancake just after flipping it, allowing it to melt into gooey perfection before serving.
*   **Spicy Pajeon:** Increase the gochugaru in the batter or add more fresh chilies for those who love extra heat.

Armed with this comprehensive Haemul Pajeon recipe, you're ready to embark on a delicious culinary journey right in your own kitchen. Whether you stick to the classic or experiment with variations, this savory Korean seafood pancake is sure to become a cherished part of your home cooking repertoire. Enjoy the crispy edges, the tender interior, and the burst of fresh seafood and green onion flavors that make Haemul Pajeon an unforgettable dish.
